decision_summary: The current design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. This is the highest priority lever for improving lateral heat spreading and reducing peak temperature.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader conductivity to its upper bound enhances lateral heat diffusion, lowering the peak temperature in the chip.

expected_effects: Improved thermal uniformity across the spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches material limits
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications (though not modeled here)
